what_ddr_is.pathway: DNA Damage Response (DDR) — the cellular machinery that detects and repairs DNA damage

key_kinases.ATR: ATR (Ataxia Telangiectasia and Rad3-related) — activated by single-stranded DNA breaks and replication stress. Phosphorylates CHK1 to halt replication and allow repair.
key_kinases.WEE1: WEE1 — phosphorylates CDK1/CDK2 to prevent premature mitotic entry when DNA is damaged. WEE1 inhibition forces cells with unrepaired DNA into mitosis → mitotic catastrophe.
key_kinases.PARP: PARP1/2 — repairs single-strand breaks via base excision repair. PARP inhibition in BRCA-mutant cells causes synthetic lethality (double-strand breaks cannot be repaired by HRR).

what_ddr_is.replication_stress: Replication stress (RS) occurs when replication forks stall or collapse — common in cancer cells with oncogene activation (KRAS, MYC, CCNE1). RS-High tumors are dependent on ATR for survival — ATR inhibition kills them selectively.
what_ddr_is.why_ddr_fails_in_unselected_populations: DDR inhibitors are highly effective in the RS-High or HRR-deficient subpopulation but have no activity in RS-Low or HRR-proficient patients. Enrolling unselected populations dilutes the signal to noise — the responder ORR (40%) is hidden inside an overall ORR (17%) that looks marginal.

rs_score_algorithm.description: Replication Stress (RS) score — classifies patients as RS-High or RS-Low
rs_score_algorithm.what_it_measures: The degree of replication fork stalling and DNA damage in the tumor. RS-High tumors are dependent on ATR for survival.
rs_score_algorithm.clinical_validation: Berzosertib: RS-High ORR 40% vs RS-Low ORR 5% — 8× difference. This is the strongest biomarker-stratified signal in the ATR/DDR corpus.

trials.primary_result: ORR 17% overall; RS-High subgroup ORR 40% vs RS-Low ORR 5%

D1_biology.rationale: ATR is a genuine target in RS-High tumors — biology is sound.

D2_selection.rationale: PRIMARY FAILURE DOMAIN. RS-High gate was not applied at enrollment. RS-High ORR 40% vs RS-Low ORR 5% — 8× difference. The responder subgroup was diluted by RS-Low patients who cannot respond to ATR inhibition.

D3_architecture.rationale: Phase I/II design appropriate for signal-finding.

D6_resistance.rationale: RS-Low patients have intrinsic resistance — not acquired resistance.

trials.transfer_lesson: Apply RS-High gate at enrollment. RS score must be computed from tumor biopsy before randomization.

D1_biology.rationale: WEE1 inhibition is biologically sound in CDK2-active tumors.

D2_selection.rationale: PRIMARY FAILURE DOMAIN. PTEN-loss creates CDK2 dependency that paradoxically PROTECTS against WEE1 inhibition — PTEN-loss cells have alternative CDK2 regulation. PTEN-intact cells are dependent on WEE1 for G2/M checkpoint. Enrolling PTEN-loss patients is not just unhelpful — it is actively harmful (PFS HR 1.82).

D6_resistance.rationale: PTEN-loss is a resistance mechanism — not acquired but intrinsic. PTEN-loss cells bypass WEE1 inhibition via PI3K/AKT-mediated CDK2 regulation.

trials.transfer_lesson: PTEN status must be assessed before enrollment. PTEN-loss is a contraindication for WEE1 inhibition. This is the canonical example of a biomarker that is both predictive of benefit AND predictive of harm.

D1_biology.rationale: ATR+PARP combination is biologically sound — ATR inhibition creates replication stress that PARP cannot repair.

D2_selection.rationale: PRIMARY FAILURE DOMAIN. Post-PARPi patients have acquired resistance mechanisms that bypass both ATR and PARP inhibition. PARPi-naive patients have intact HRR machinery that is sensitized by ATR inhibition. Enrolling post-PARPi patients dilutes the signal from PARPi-naive patients (ORR 36% vs 4%).

D6_resistance.rationale: Post-PARPi resistance is the dominant failure mechanism — BRCA reversion mutations, ABCB1 upregulation, and RAD51 restoration all bypass the ATR+PARP combination.

trials.transfer_lesson: PARPi-naive status must be a mandatory enrollment criterion for ATR+PARP combinations. Post-PARPi patients should be excluded or enrolled in a separate arm with a different combination strategy.

trials.primary_result: STK11/KEAP1-loss subgroup showed benefit; STK11-intact did not. Preliminary data.

latify_two_layer_demonstration.description: LATIFY is the ONLY receipted two-layer demonstration of CrisPRO's mechanism fit engine
latify_two_layer_demonstration.layer_1_result: Target-Lock: ceralasertib targets ATR — confirmed genuine driver in STK11/KEAP1-mutant NSCLC (replication stress elevated)
latify_two_layer_demonstration.layer_2_result: Mechanism Fit: responder score 0.8936 vs non-responder score 0.6295
latify_two_layer_demonstration.delta_status: QUARANTINED — CT-03 blocker: patent delta +0.366 vs receipt delta +0.2641 — version conflict unresolved. Do not cite any specific delta value.
latify_two_layer_demonstration.what_to_cite: Responder score 0.8936 vs non-responder 0.6295 — this is the correct citation

D2_selection.rationale: STK11/KEAP1 gate identifies the responder subpopulation. STK11-intact patients do not respond — the gate is necessary.

D5_combination.rationale: ATRi + IO combination is mechanistically sound — ATR inhibition increases neoantigen presentation and PD-L1 expression, sensitizing tumors to checkpoint blockade.

trials.transfer_lesson: STK11/KEAP1 mutation status must be assessed before enrollment. The two-layer demonstration shows CrisPRO can identify the responder subpopulation before the trial reads out.

D3_architecture.rationale: Phase I dose-finding — not designed to detect efficacy signal.

trials.transfer_lesson: Dose-finding data establishes the safety profile for Phase II. The CAPRI Phase II is the efficacy read-out.

what_crispro_proved: Berzosertib: RS-High ORR 40% vs RS-Low ORR 5% — 8× difference; D2 Selection failure decoded
Adavosertib: PTEN-intact ORR 23% vs PTEN-loss ORR 0%; PFS HR 0.55 vs 1.82 — PTEN-loss is HARMFUL
CAPRI: PARPi-naive ORR 36% vs post-PARPi ORR 4%; PFS HR 0.42 vs 1.31 — post-PARPi is HARMFUL
GLB set: 4 ATR/DDR trials representing $1.95B in failed program investment decoded
LATIFY: responder score 0.8936 vs non-responder 0.6295 — only receipted two-layer demonstration (delta QUARANTINED)
what_crispro_can_offer: For any ATR/DDR abstract: identify which biomarker gate is missing (RS-High, PTEN-intact, PARPi-naive) and what the responder ORR would be with correct patient selection. For new DDR programs: apply the 8D framework to predict the failure mode before enrollment. CrisPRO has decoded $1.95B in failed ATR/DDR investment.
caveats: LATIFY delta is QUARANTINED — do not cite a specific delta value
GLB set IP valuation figures are benchmarks, not guarantees
RS score algorithm is not publicly available — CrisPRO uses a proprietary implementation
